message.content:
Internal Spearphishing involves adversaries using compromised internal email accounts to target other users within the same organization. This technique leverages the trust associated with internal communications to increase the likelihood of successful phishing attempts. Adversaries may control the email account by either compromising the user's device with malware or stealing account credentials. Common methods include sending spearphishing attachments or links that deliver malicious payloads or redirect to credential-harvesting sites.

The impact of internal spearphishing can be significant, leading to further compromise of sensitive information, lateral movement within the network, and broader organizational breaches. Notable incidents include campaigns by the Eye Pyramid, Syrian Electronic Army, Gamaredon Group, HEXANE, Kimsuky, Lazarus Group, and Leviathan, which have used this technique to compromise thousands of accounts and gain deeper access within targeted organizations.

Mitigation strategies include implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) to protect email accounts, conducting regular security awareness training to help users recognize phishing attempts, and deploying advanced email filtering solutions to detect and block malicious emails. Additionally, monitoring for unusual email activity and promptly responding to suspected compromises can help limit the impact of internal spearphishing attacks.
